# How to Make Our Ideas Clear

*[Charles Sanders Peirce](../philosophers/peirce.md) · 1878 · *How to Make Our Ideas Clear**

The founding essay of pragmatism, where Peirce states his maxim: to clarify an idea, consider what conceivable practical effects its object would have; the whole meaning of the conception lies in those effects. Truth is there defined as the opinion fated to be agreed upon at the end of inquiry.
